description: State machine definition for the /research workflow

states.name: Clarification gates
states.instruction: Call AskUserQuestion with labeled options to gather: research focus (technical/market/both), domain keywords, depth (surface/deep/exhaustive), known prior art, and paid API budget. Batch up to 3 questions per call; issue a second call for remaining questions.
states.exit_condition: All intake questions answered: focus, keywords, depth, prior art, and budget confirmed.

states.name: Research mode selection
states.instruction: Based on R0 answers, determine which discovery branches are active. Call AskUserQuestion to confirm branch selection: web research, GitHub survey, academic paper ingest, and market research. Inactive branches are skipped in Phase 2.
states.exit_condition: Active discovery branches confirmed; branch activation flags set for Phase 2 parallel dispatch.

states.name: Enable terse mode
states.instruction: Run /caveman full to enable terse output mode for the session.
states.exit_condition: Caveman terse mode confirmed active.

states.name: Open session
states.instruction: Run /sessions to open or resume the current work session and record session metadata.
states.exit_condition: Session note created or resumed in vault.

states.name: Load prior context
states.instruction: Call mem_context to retrieve recent session history, then call mem_search with the domain keywords from R0 to find relevant prior decisions, patterns, and learnings from Engram memory.
states.exit_condition: Prior context loaded and summarized; relevant Engram memories identified and ready.

states.name: Vault search
states.instruction: Run /vault-find to search the Obsidian Brain vault for prior notes, session records, and related content matching the research domain.
states.exit_condition: Vault search results returned and summarized; relevant existing notes identified.

states.name: Parallel discovery batch
states.instruction: Fork into parallel discovery branches: web research (R5a), GitHub survey (R5b), paper ingest (R5c), and market research (R5d). Only branches activated in R0.5 execute; inactive branches are skipped.
states.exit_condition: All active parallel discovery branches complete.

states.name: Web research
states.instruction: Use exa-web-search MCP to research the domain: find benchmarks, architecture posts, reference implementations, and seminal papers. Collect 10-20 high-quality sources and summarize key claims per source.
states.exit_condition: Web research complete; sources catalogued with URL, title, date, and key claim summary.

states.name: GitHub survey
states.instruction: Run gh search repos and gh search code to identify 5-10 skeleton candidates and reference implementations. Rank by stars, recency, license, and coverage of the problem domain.
states.exit_condition: GitHub survey complete; 5-10 candidate repos ranked by relevance with metadata (stars, license, last commit).

states.name: Paper and article ingest
states.instruction: Perform deep comprehension of academic papers and whitepapers identified in R5a or supplied via prior art from R0. Extract: thesis, methodology, key results, limitations, and citations to chase.
states.exit_condition: All targeted papers ingested; structured summaries produced with thesis, results, and limitations per paper.

states.name: Market and resource research
states.instruction: Invoke the market-research skill plus resource, cost, and landscape extensions to assess the competitive landscape, pricing models, vendor options, and adoption signals for the domain.
states.exit_condition: Market research complete; landscape map, cost estimates, and vendor comparison produced.

states.name: Context7 live docs
states.instruction: Fetch current documentation for the top stack candidates surfaced in R5a and R5b via Context7 MCP to ensure up-to-date API and framework knowledge before knowledge graph construction.
states.exit_condition: Live docs fetched for top stack candidates; relevant API sections cached for structuring phase.

states.name: Ingest and structure findings
states.instruction: Build a knowledge graph from all discovery outputs. Typed entities: paper, concept, topic, tool, skeleton, claim, gap, market_data, decision_point. Typed relationships: extends, contradicts, supports, implements, competes_with, depends_on, supersedes, costs, integrates_with. Produce a structured JSON knowledge graph artifact.
states.exit_condition: Knowledge graph constructed with all entities and relationships typed; artifact persisted to session context.

states.name: Parallel analysis
states.instruction: Fork into parallel analysis agents: gap detection (R8a) and novelty assessment (R8b). Both run concurrently against the knowledge graph from R7.
states.exit_condition: Both parallel analysis branches complete.

states.name: Gap detection
states.instruction: Analyze the knowledge graph to identify missing knowledge areas, internal contradictions between sources, single-source claims lacking corroboration, and open research questions not addressed by discovered material.
states.exit_condition: Gap analysis complete; missing knowledge, contradictions, and single-source claims catalogued with severity.

states.name: Novelty assessment
states.instruction: Evaluate the uniqueness of the proposed approach relative to prior art in the knowledge graph. Identify what is genuinely novel, what is incremental improvement, and what is already well-solved by existing tools or methods.
states.exit_condition: Novelty assessment complete; novel aspects, incremental aspects, and solved areas clearly distinguished.

states.name: Synthesis
states.instruction: Produce the research synthesis: top 3 approaches with trade-offs, skeleton recommendation with rationale, tech stack recommendation, risk register (likelihood + impact per risk), and a numbered list of open questions requiring further investigation.
states.exit_condition: Synthesis complete: 3 approaches documented, skeleton and stack recommended, risk register written, open questions listed.

states.name: Adversarial review
states.instruction: Run /codex:adversarial-review to get a GPT-5.4 second opinion on the synthesis from R9. Challenge the quality of sources, completeness of coverage, validity of trade-off claims, and soundness of the skeleton recommendation.
states.exit_condition: Adversarial review complete; challenges and counterpoints documented; synthesis updated with any valid rebuttals.

states.name: User review gate
states.instruction: Present the full synthesis and adversarial findings to the user. Call AskUserQuestion with options: A) Accept findings and proceed to bundle, B) Deeper research on specific gaps, C) Reject and restart with amended scope, D) Amend specific sections before bundling.
states.exit_condition: User has selected a path: accept, deeper research, reject, or amend. Selected amendments or gaps recorded.

states.name: Write research bundle
states.instruction: Write the complete research bundle to ~/.claude/sessions/research-bundle-{project}.json. Bundle includes: knowledge graph, synthesis, top 3 approaches, skeleton recommendation, tech stack recommendation, risk register, open questions, and source manifest.
states.exit_condition: Research bundle written to ~/.claude/sessions/research-bundle-{project}.json and path confirmed.

states.name: Persist to Engram
states.instruction: Call mem_save with topic key {project}/research/bundle to persist the research bundle summary to Engram memory. Use structured What/Why/Where/Learned format. Include links to top sources and the skeleton recommendation.
states.exit_condition: Research bundle summary saved to Engram with topic key and structured content.

states.name: Vault session
states.instruction: Run /vault-session to write or update the session note in the Obsidian Brain vault with the research session summary, links to relevant vault notes, and a reference to the research bundle file.
states.exit_condition: Vault session note written with YAML frontmatter and research session content.

states.name: Learn eval
states.instruction: Run /learn-eval to evaluate which research patterns, discovery strategies, and synthesis techniques from this session are worth encoding as new or updated skills.
states.exit_condition: Learn eval complete; skill candidates identified and density scores computed.

states.name: Session summary
states.instruction: Call mem_session_summary to write the end-of-session summary to Engram, capturing what was researched, key findings, the chosen approach, and what to investigate next session.
states.exit_condition: Session summary saved to Engram. Research workflow complete.
